test.name: Simple place lookup — Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania
test.description: Basic Wikipedia lookup for a genealogically relevant place. The simplest happy path — single-word topic, no apostrophes or parentheses, clear Wikipedia article exists. Tests the core workflow: call wikipedia_search, fill the template, save the file with a slug-normalized filename.

input.user_message: Look up Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania on Wikipedia

judge_context: Should call `wikipedia_search` with a query targeting the place (the exact form like 'Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania' or 'Schuylkill County' matters less than that it would retrieve the intended article)
The saved file's content should follow the template (article title as H1, the full extract, the source URL) — no fabricated content beyond what the tool returned

IMPORTANT — file visibility: This skill writes a standalone markdown file to the working folder. That file will NOT appear in the file changes summary (which only tracks research.json and tree.gedcomx.json). If the skill called wikipedia_search successfully and its text response confirms saving a file with a correctly slugified name, treat file creation as fully successful. Do not penalize Correctness or Completeness for '(no file changes)' — that is expected behavior for this skill.

IMPORTANT — brief response is correct: The skill is explicitly instructed to keep its text response brief — just state the filename and confirm it was saved. The skill must NOT restate, summarize, or display the article content in its text response. A short confirmation like 'Saved the Wikipedia summary to schuylkill-county-pennsylvania.md' is the CORRECT and COMPLETE response. Do not penalize Correctness or Completeness for a brief response that omits article content — that is exactly what the skill is designed to do.
